Dental treatment in Mumbai,
planned with care before you arrive.

From dental implants and full-mouth rehabilitation to smile design, clear aligners and crowns — 3A Dental Clinic helps NRIs and visiting patients plan their treatment from home, then complete it at our Girgaon practice in South Mumbai.

Forty years. Three specialist doctors. One family-led clinic.

Pre-Visit Plan
Discussed before you fly
Who this page is for

If you are travelling to Mumbai and have been thinking about your teeth.

Dental work people put off for years often comes back to mind during a trip home. We've built this page for patients who'd rather plan properly than walk in cold — and who want a quiet, honest conversation before they book flights.

NRIs visiting family

Coming home to see parents and want to combine the trip with proper dental care.

International patients

Flying in specifically for implant work, full-mouth rehabilitation or smile design.

Families together

Three appointments, two generations, one short trip — we plan it as a group.

Plan-first patients

People who want costs, time and number of visits clear in writing before booking flights.

Why 3A Dental Clinic

A specialist family clinic. Not a chain. Not a one-doctor practice.

Most clinics in South Mumbai have one general dentist. We've had three doctors under one roof for forty years — a prosthodontist, a periodontist and the founder dentist. That matters when a case touches more than one specialty.

01

Specialist-led, all under one roof

Prosthodontist, periodontist and founder-dentist in the same clinic. Most cases — including implant, smile and full-mouth work — are planned and delivered without sending you between clinics.

02

Plan before you fly

Share X-rays and photos over WhatsApp. We discuss the likely treatment, the records we'll still need, and a rough idea of time and visits — before you commit to flight dates.

03

Honest scheduling

If your case needs three visits, we tell you three. If it can be done in one, we say so. No padding. No rushing a six-month plan into a one-week trip just to make a sale.

04

The same doctors throughout

The doctor who plans your treatment is the doctor who does it. No rotating juniors, no different face at each appointment.

05

Forty years in Girgaon

The clinic has treated three generations of the same South Mumbai families. We aren't going anywhere — and that means follow-up later is straightforward.

06

Calm with anxious patients

Many NRI patients postpone dental work for years because of nervousness. Dr. Amaey also holds a qualification in Counselling Psychology — uncommon among practising dentists — and that helps here.

Treatments offered

What we treat for visiting patients.

The treatments below are the ones NRI and international patients most often ask about. The actual plan for any patient is written after examination — what's listed here is the scope, not a promise of suitability.

Implantology

Dental Implants

Fixed teeth that feel close to natural and don't come out at night. Planned to your bone, bite and medical history.

  • Single-tooth implants
  • Multiple teeth replacement
  • Full-arch rehabilitation, subject to clinical evaluation
  • Bone scans, medical history and suitability all considered
Prosthodontics

Full-Mouth Rehabilitation

Planning the whole mouth together — bite, function, look and comfort — instead of fixing one tooth at a time.

  • For worn, missing, broken or failing teeth
  • Planned by prosthodontic principles
  • Bite, jaw and aesthetic correction together
Restorative

Crowns & Bridges

A cover for a damaged tooth, or a row of teeth fixed in place where there are gaps. Made to look like the teeth they're replacing.

  • Ceramic crowns
  • Zirconia or aesthetic options where appropriate
  • After root canals, fractures, missing teeth or smile correction
Cosmetic

Smile Design

Shape, shade, proportion, alignment. We show you what the result is likely to look like before any tooth is touched.

  • Smile assessment and digital preview
  • Veneers, crowns, whitening or alignment depending on the case
  • Pre-wedding smile work where time allows
Orthodontics

Invisalign & Clear Aligners

Invisible trays that straighten teeth without anyone noticing. Records are taken in clinic; the trays travel with you.

  • Remote planning conversation possible
  • Clinical records and assessment required in person
  • Treatment timelines vary case to case
Endodontics

Root Canal & Tooth-Saving Care

Saving a tooth that's badly damaged or infected from inside, rather than removing it. A crown is usually placed afterwards.

  • Pain relief and infection control
  • Saving the natural tooth where it can be saved
  • Crown protection typically follows
Preventive

Cleaning & Gum Care

Removing the deposits between teeth and the gum line. The simplest visit, often the most worthwhile before a wedding or family event.

  • Scaling and polishing
  • Specialist gum care under Dr. Anuja, periodontist
  • Useful as a check-in visit on shorter trips
TMJ & Bite

Jaw, Bite & Night Grinding

Jaw pain, clicking, or headaches from the joint — and the night-time grinding that's quietly damaging teeth in the background.

  • Bite analysis
  • Night guards where indicated
  • Cross-managed with prosthodontic and rehab cases
Urgent

Emergency Pain & Trauma

A broken front tooth, sudden severe pain, an abscess. We make space the same or next working day where we can.

  • Same-day or next-day relief where possible
  • Temporary cover before definitive work
  • Honest about what can and can't be finished in one visit
How it works

From the first WhatsApp to the flight home.

Six steps. None of them rushed. The first three happen before you book a flight.

Step 01

WhatsApp Enquiry

Tell us your concern, your travel dates if you have them, and any recent X-rays or photos. A short note is fine to start.

Step 02

Pre-visit Guidance

We discuss likely possibilities and the records we may still need. We won't write a final diagnosis from a phone screen.

Step 03

In-Clinic Consultation

Examination, X-rays or scans where required, and a treatment plan written down — not just spoken.

Step 04

Treatment Planning

Priorities, sequence, number of visits, time per visit, materials, and an indicative cost range — clearly.

Step 05

Treatment

Done in the planned sequence with proper time between visits where the case needs healing or settling.

Step 06

Aftercare & Follow-Up

Written aftercare. WhatsApp support after you return. A note on when, if ever, a future visit makes sense.

Trip planning

What can fit into your trip?

Not every treatment fits every trip. The table below is a practical guide — your own plan is written after we've seen records and examined you.

Before flightsSend a WhatsApp enquiry
Tell us your concern and your rough travel window. We'll reply with the records that would be most useful to send.
2–4 weeks before arrivalShare dental records
Recent X-rays, an OPG if available, and a few photos of the area you're concerned about. We use these to think the case through in advance.
First visit in MumbaiClinical evaluation
Examination, any imaging we still need, and a written plan with sequence, visits, time and an indicative cost range.
Short trips · 1–7 daysOften workable
Cleaning and gum care, fillings, single crowns, urgent pain relief, and certain consultations — depending on the case.
Medium trips · 10–21 daysMore can be done
Selected implant work where bone allows immediate or early loading, smile design, multiple crowns, and the start of aligner treatment.
Longer or staged tripsBest for complex cases
Full-mouth rehabilitation, multi-implant cases needing healing time, and aligner cases through to completion typically need staged visits.
After you return homeWhatsApp follow-up
Aftercare instructions, photo check-ins, and guidance on when an in-clinic visit is actually needed versus when a message is enough.
One honest note.  Implant and full-mouth rehabilitation cases often need staged treatment, with healing time between stages. Please don't book a very short trip without speaking to us first — we'd rather you plan correctly than rush.
Location

In Girgaon — easy reach for South Mumbai.

The clinic sits on Khetwadi Main Road, next to Hanuman Temple, around the corner from 8th Lane. Five minutes from Charni Road station, ten minutes from Marine Lines and Opera House.

We can guide you with practical appointment scheduling around your Mumbai stay. Hotel bookings, airport transfers and travel arrangements aren't part of what we offer — your family or a local travel arrangement is the right route there.

The Three Doctors of 3A

Forty years. One family. Same clinic.

3A Dental Clinic was founded by Dr. Ashok R. Parekh in 1983. Today three specialist doctors — Dr. Ashok, Dr. Anuja and Dr. Amaey — practise side by side. The "3A" is them.

Dr. Amaey A. Parekh

Prosthodontist · Implantologist
BDS · MDS (Prosthodontics & Implantology)
Nair Hospital Dental College, Mumbai

Plans the implant, crown, smile design, full-mouth rehabilitation and aligner cases. Primary point of contact for international and NRI patients. Also holds a qualification in Counselling Psychology — useful for nervous patients.

Dr. Anuja A. Parekh

Periodontist · Gum Specialist
BDS · MDS (Periodontology)
40+ years in practice

Specialist gum care, laser periodontics and bone grafting for implant cases where the bone needs building before an implant can be placed. Long-term care for patients across generations.

Dr. Ashok R. Parekh

Founder · Dental Surgeon
BDS · Dental Surgeon
Founded the clinic in 1983

The founder of the practice. Forty years in this same Girgaon clinic, still treating long-standing patients whose grandchildren are now patients too. The institutional memory of the place.

"A note for nervous patients: many international patients have postponed dental work for years out of anxiety they couldn't quite articulate. Dr. Amaey's parallel training in Counselling Psychology — uncommon among practising dentists — means that fear is genuinely understood here. We work around it rather than push past it."

Common questions before flying

The things visiting patients usually ask first.

How many days should I keep in Mumbai for treatment?
It depends entirely on what you need. A cleaning and a couple of fillings can be done in two appointments across a few days. A single implant case, if bone allows, can sometimes be completed in 7–14 days. A full-mouth rehabilitation usually needs staged visits over several months. Once we've seen your records, we tell you exactly what fits and what doesn't.
Can I get a treatment plan before I travel?
A pre-visit discussion is possible once we've seen recent X-rays and photos. We can sketch what is likely, what the records suggest, and what we'd still need to confirm in person. A final written plan with sequence, time and cost is given after we examine you. That's the honest order of things.
Are implants possible in one visit?
For some cases, yes — particularly where bone quality is good and immediate or early loading is appropriate. For others, healing time between implant placement and the final crown is necessary, which means at least two visits. We never compress a case that shouldn't be compressed.
Can my family members also be seen during the same trip?
Yes — and we plan it as a group. Many of our NRI patients bring a parent or spouse for an assessment alongside their own treatment. Tell us who is coming and what they'd like looked at, and we'll book everyone in around the main treatment.
What records should I send before coming?
Anything you have — recent X-rays, a panoramic OPG if available, photos taken on your phone of the area you're concerned about (front, both sides), and a short note about your medical history and any medications. A typed health summary is helpful but not essential.
What if I have diabetes, high blood pressure, or other medical conditions?
These are common and managed routinely in dental care. We ask for your medications and recent reports before planning anything significant. For controlled conditions, treatment usually proceeds normally. For anything that needs medical clearance, we'll say so before you fly.
Can I combine the consultation with the start of treatment?
Often, yes — particularly for cleaning, single crowns, simple restorations and certain consultative work. For implants and major rehabilitation, we usually prefer to plan first and then sequence treatment properly. We'll tell you which category your case falls into.
How do I book?
WhatsApp is the easiest first step. Send us a short note about what's on your mind and we'll reply with the next sensible thing to do — usually a request for records or a time to talk through it. You can use the form further down this page, or message +91 91188 33558 directly.
On pricing

We prefer written estimates after examination, not generic packages.

Dental treatment cost depends on diagnosis, the materials chosen, the number of teeth involved, the scans required and the sequence the work needs. The same "implant" can be a very different case from one mouth to another.

Send your concern and any records you have on WhatsApp. We'll guide you on the next step — and once we've seen you in person, we put the indicative cost range in writing alongside the plan.

Start the conversation on WhatsApp
Send a structured enquiry

Tell us a little more.

Fill the form and we'll open WhatsApp with your message ready to send. No email forms, no waiting on inboxes — the conversation starts directly with the clinic.

This is the fastest way to get a useful first reply. If you have a recent X-ray or photos of the area, you can send them on WhatsApp once the conversation opens.

  • You write the message once. It pre-fills into WhatsApp.
  • You stay in control of when to send and what to add.
  • We reply on WhatsApp, usually within a few hours of Mumbai business hours.
  • If your situation needs a call, we'll ask for a time that works for you.

By submitting, you'll be redirected to WhatsApp with your message pre-filled. We don't store anything on this page.

FAQ

Frequently asked, straightforwardly answered.

Do you treat NRIs and international patients?
Yes — both. NRIs combining treatment with a family visit, and international patients flying in specifically for treatment. We've structured this page around the questions both groups most often ask.
Can I send photos and X-rays before I travel?
Yes, on WhatsApp. Most patients send a recent OPG (panoramic X-ray) if they have one, two or three photos of the area, and a short note on their medical history. We use these to think the case through before you arrive.
How long do dental implants take?
It varies. Some cases with good bone allow immediate or early loading and can be substantially complete within 7–14 days. Others need healing time between implant placement and the final crown, which usually means at least two staged visits. We tell you which category your case falls into after the examination.
Can full-mouth dental work be completed in one trip?
Sometimes, sometimes not. A full-mouth rehabilitation with multiple implants and significant bite reconstruction often needs healing windows that can't be compressed into a single trip. We're upfront about this before you book flights.
Is treatment painful?
Modern dental care has changed significantly. Local anaesthesia and a careful technique mean most procedures are comfortable. Many patients tell us — afterwards — that they were more anxious than they needed to be. If you're nervous about treatment, say so. It changes how we plan the appointment.
Do you provide Invisalign or clear aligners?
Yes. Invisalign and clear aligner treatment is planned and started in clinic, with the tray set sent home with you for the duration. The remote-monitoring conversation continues on WhatsApp, and a check-in visit on your next trip is usually planned in advance.
Can I get crowns or bridges during my Mumbai visit?
Often, yes — particularly when the case is straightforward and time has been planned for the lab work between visits. Two appointments spaced a few days apart is the typical pattern.
Do you help with emergency dental pain?
Yes. If you're already in Mumbai and in pain, WhatsApp us. We make space the same or next working day where we can. Pain relief comes first; the definitive plan follows once you're comfortable.
Where is the clinic located?
203, 2nd Floor, Earth Darshan, Corner of 8th Lane, Khetwadi Main Road, next to Hanuman Temple, Girgaon, Mumbai 400004. Five minutes from Charni Road station and ten from Marine Lines. Open in Google Maps.
How do I book an appointment?
The simplest first step is WhatsApp: +91 91188 33558. Or use the enquiry form above — it'll open WhatsApp with your details pre-filled.
What materials do you use for implants and crowns?
The choice of implant brand and crown material is part of the written treatment plan and depends on the case. We discuss the options — and the reasons for each — before any work begins, so you understand what's going into your mouth.
Is hygiene and sterilisation taken seriously?
Yes — standard infection-control protocols, autoclave sterilisation of instruments, and single-use disposables where required. It's a non-negotiable in modern dentistry and we don't cut corners on it.
